scope: Make the deeper-rerun decision explicit after the medium checkpoint and stop condition

decision_context.prior_state: The repo had a canonical medium checkpoint, a committed stop-condition note, and an operator handoff, but the actual rerun decision was still distributed across notes and terminal history.
decision_context.decision: Defer any deeper full-universe rerun until it has a declared harness, runtime budget, and success criterion.
decision_context.why_now: Without an explicit rerun decision note, the next operator could still treat another blind rerun as the default next step.

files_expected.purpose: States the current decision to defer another deeper rerun and defines the contract for reopening that path.
Captures why the rerun decision needed to become a first-class artifact.
Appends the rerun-decision tranche to the transparent change log.
Adds the rerun-decision tranche to the day-level diff summary.

planned_steps: State whether another deeper rerun should happen from the current March 24 state
Record the reasoning from the medium checkpoint and failed runtime experiment
Define the minimum contract required before reopening the deeper-rerun path

expected_checks: The note explicitly says whether another rerun should happen now
The note defines concrete reopen criteria rather than only expressing caution
The dashboard rule remains downstream of the rerun decision

guardrails: Do not imply that the medium checkpoint is a final trusted portfolio verdict
Do not reopen speculative runtime work without a profiler-backed hypothesis
Keep the rerun decision grounded in the actual March 24 artifact set
